title: Pfaffian
summary: Defines Pfaffian-like symbolic invariants and the P_l_n family used in the Gauss–Bonnet-type application.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines Pfaffian-like symbolic invariants and the P_l_n family used in the Gauss–Bonnet-type application.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Invariants/Pfaffian.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The scalar invariant Pf_ell(W). In explicit terms, for ell in the natural numbers, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.pf Like ell Weyl Tensor Invariant.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals -2 times ell : Int parity : equals Invariant Parity.even, rfl ⟩.

The corresponding ambient invariant Pf_ell(RmTilde). In explicit terms, for ell in the natural numbers, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.pf Like ell Ambient Riemann Tensor Invariant.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals -2 times ell : Int parity : equals Invariant Parity.even, rfl ⟩.
For ell in the natural numbers, Weyl Pf Like ell.1.weight equals -2 times ell : Int.
For ell in the natural numbers, Ambient Rm Pf Like ell.1.weight equals -2 times ell : Int.

Semantic input that the alternating complete contraction preserves association. Its mathematical data are: associated: for every ell : Nat, Invariant Evaluation Model.Associated Weyl Pf Like ell.1 Ambient Rm Pf Like ell.1.
Pfaffian-like Weyl polynomials are straightenable. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem WeylPfLike_straightenable [InvariantEvaluationModel] [PfLikeStraightness] (ell : Nat) : ScalarStraightenable (WeylPfLike ell) (AmbientRmPfLike ell)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The scalar conformal invariant P_(ell,n) = i^*(DeltaTilde^(n/2-ell) Pf_ell(RmTilde)). In explicit terms, for ell, n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is restrict To Einstein Slice ambient Laplacian Iter n divided by 2 minus ell Ambient Rm Pf Like ell.
For ell, n in the natural numbers, hn of type Even n, and hRange of type ell < equals n divided by 2, P l n ell n.1.weight equals - n : Int.
correspondence.leanType: theorem P_l_n_weight (ell n : Nat) (hn : Even n) (hRange : ell <= n / 2) : (P_l_n ell n).1.weight = -(n : Int)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For ell, n in the natural numbers, hn of type Even n, and hRange of type ell < equals n divided by 2, Is Scalar Conformal Invariant P l n ell n.
correspondence.leanType: theorem P_l_n_conformal [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] (ell n : Nat) (hn : Even n) (hRange : ell <= n / 2) : IsScalarConformalInvariant (P_l_n ell n)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The full Pfaffian in even formal dimension n. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.pf Like n divided by 2 Tensor Invariant Expr.riem rank : equals 0 weight : equals - n : Int parity : equals Invariant Parity.even, rfl ⟩.

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
